text.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP)
text.summary: MCP is a protocol for connecting Claude Desktop to external tools, resources, and prompt templates; for programmatic API use, standard tool use patterns are preferred over MCP.

section.title: MCP overview

rule.statement: MCP is primarily designed for Claude Desktop (stdio transport); programmatic API usage should use standard tool use, not MCP.
rule.rationale: MCP servers communicate via stdio by default and require subprocess lifecycle management; standard tool use is stateless and easier to operationalize in servers.

section.p: MCP uses three primitives: Tools (actions), Resources (read-only data), Prompts (reusable templates).

example.note: Available official servers: filesystem, github, postgres, sqlite, puppeteer, google-drive, slack, brave-search.
